per_example.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.creation
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.creation FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_id = T3.head_id WHERE T3.born_state = 'Alabama'

per_example.context: CREATE TABLE department (creation VARCHAR, department_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE management (department_id VARCHAR, head_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE head (head_id VARCHAR, born_state VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT d.name, d.num_employees
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.num_employees FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id WHERE T2.temporary_acting = 'Yes'
per_example.question: Show the name and number of employees for the departments managed by heads whose temporary acting value is 'Yes'?
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE management (department_id VARCHAR, temporary_acting VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE department (name VARCHAR, num_employees VARCHAR, department_id VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(d.department_id) FROM department d LEFT JOIN management m ON d.department_id = m.department_id WHERE m.department_id IS NULL

per_example.question: How many departments are led by heads who are not mentioned?
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE management (department_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE department (department_id VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.age FROM head AS T1 INNER JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.head_id = T2.head_id WHERE T2.temporary_acting = 'acting'
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.age FROM management AS T2 JOIN head AS T1 ON T1.head_id = T2.head_id WHERE T2.temporary_acting = 'Yes'

per_example.context: CREATE TABLE head (age VARCHAR, head_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE management (head_id VARCHAR, temporary_acting VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT t1.born_state
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T3.born_state FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_id = T3.head_id WHERE T1.name = 'Treasury' INTERSECT SELECT T3.born_state FROM department AS T1 JOIN management AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id JOIN head AS T3 ON T2.head_id = T3.head_id WHERE T1.name = 'Homeland Security'
per_example.question: List the states where both the secretary of 'Treasury' department and the secretary of 'Homeland Security' were born.
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE management (department_id VARCHAR, head_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE head (born_state VARCHAR, head_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE department (department_id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT department_id, name, COUNT(*) as num_heads
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.department_id, T1.name, COUNT(*) FROM management AS T2 JOIN department AS T1 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
per_example.question: Which department has more than 1 head at a time? List the id, name and the number of heads.
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE management (department_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE department (department_id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR)

per_example.context: CREATE TABLE farm_competition (Host_city_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE city (Official_Name VARCHAR, City_ID VARCHAR)

per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.Status FROM city AS T1 JOIN farm_competition AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_city_ID GROUP BY T2.Host_city_ID ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT T2.Theme

per_example.gold_sql: SELECT Official_Name FROM city WHERE NOT City_ID IN (SELECT Host_city_ID FROM farm_competition)

per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.student_details FROM students AS T1 JOIN student_course_registrations AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T3.course_name, COUNT(*) FROM students AS T1 JOIN student_course_registrations AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T2.course_id

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT s.lat, s.long, s.city

per_example.context: CREATE TABLE trip (start_station_id VARCHAR, duration VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE station (lat VARCHAR, long VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, id VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT id FROM station WHERE city = 'San Francisco' GROUP BY id HAVING AVG(bikes_available) > 10

per_example.context: CREATE TABLE status (id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER); CREATE TABLE station (id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT s.name, s.id
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.id FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T2.station_id HAVING AVG(T2.bikes_available) > 14 UNION SELECT name, id FROM station WHERE installation_date LIKE "12/%"
per_example.question: What are the names and ids of stations that had more than 14 bikes available on average or were installed in December?
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR, installation_date VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE status (station_id VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT AVG(bikes_available) FROM station WHERE city != 'Palo Alto'

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT AVG(long) FROM station WHERE bikes_available <= 10

per_example.context: CREATE TABLE station (long INTEGER, id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER); CREATE TABLE status (long INTEGER, id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT date, zip_code
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT date, zip_code FROM weather WHERE min_dew_point_f < (SELECT MIN(min_dew_point_f) FROM weather WHERE zip_code = 94107)

per_example.context: CREATE TABLE weather (date VARCHAR, zip_code VARCHAR, min_dew_point_f INTEGER)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT T2.installation_date

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(end_station_id)

per_example.gold_sql: SELECT id FROM station WHERE lat > 37.4 EXCEPT SELECT station_id FROM status GROUP BY station_id HAVING MIN(bikes_available) < 7
per_example.question: What are the ids of stations that have latitude above 37.4 and never had bike availability below 7?
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE status (id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, lat INTEGER, bikes_available INTEGER); CREATE TABLE station (id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, lat INTEGER, bikes_available INTEGER)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT name FROM station WHERE id IN (SELECT station_id FROM status WHERE station_id IN (SELECT id FROM station WHERE city != 'San Jose' AND id IN (SELECT station_id FROM station WHERE bikes_available > 10)))
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id GROUP BY T2.station_id HAVING AVG(bikes_available) > 10 EXCEPT SELECT name FROM station WHERE city = "San Jose"
per_example.question: What are names of stations that have average bike availability above 10 and are not located in San Jose city?
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE status (station_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT T1.long, AVG(T2.duration) FROM station AS T1 INNER JOIN trip AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.start_station_id GROUP BY T1.id
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.long, AVG(T2.duration) FROM station AS T1 JOIN trip AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.start_station_id GROUP BY T2.start_station_id
per_example.question: For each station, return its longitude and the average duration of trips that started from the station.
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, long VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE trip (duration INTEGER, start_station_id VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT T2.lat, MIN(T1.duration) FROM trip AS T1 INNER JOIN station AS T2 ON T1.end_station_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.id
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.lat, MIN(T2.duration) FROM station AS T1 JOIN trip AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.end_station_id GROUP BY T2.end_station_id
per_example.question: For each station, find its latitude and the minimum duration of trips that ended at the station.
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE trip (duration INTEGER, end_station_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, lat VARCHAR, id VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT zip_code FROM weather WHERE max_dew_point_f != '70'
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT zip_code FROM weather EXCEPT SELECT DISTINCT zip_code FROM weather WHERE max_dew_point_f >= 70
per_example.question: Find all the zip codes in which the max dew point have never reached 70.
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE weather (zip_code VARCHAR, max_dew_point_f VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT id FROM trip WHERE duration >= (SELECT AVG(duration) FROM trip WHERE zip_code = '94103')
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT id FROM trip WHERE duration >= (SELECT AVG(duration) FROM trip WHERE zip_code = 94103)
per_example.question: Find the id for the trips that lasted at least as long as the average duration of trips in zip code 94103.
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE trip (id VARCHAR, duration INTEGER, zip_code VARCHAR)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT s.id, s.name
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.id, T1.name FROM station AS T1 JOIN status AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.station_id WHERE T2.bikes_available > 12
per_example.question: What are the id and name of the stations that have ever had more than 12 bikes available?
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE station (id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE status (station_id VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER)

per_example.generated_sql: SELECT zip_code FROM trip GROUP BY zip_code HAVING AVG(mean_humidity) < 70 AND COUNT(zip_code) >= 100
per_example.gold_sql: SELECT zip_code FROM weather GROUP BY zip_code HAVING AVG(mean_humidity) < 70 INTERSECT SELECT zip_code FROM trip GROUP BY zip_code HAVING COUNT(*) >= 100
per_example.question: Give me the zip code where the average mean humidity is below 70 and at least 100 trips took place.
per_example.context: CREATE TABLE weather (zip_code VARCHAR, mean_humidity INTEGER); CREATE TABLE trip (zip_code VARCHAR, mean_humidity INTEGER)
